question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Field: versionedConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `versionedConfiguration` is lazily initialized once and never refreshed — even if `properties` or `network` changes — to cause a stale `VersionedConfiguration` to be used for all contract calls after a configuration update, producing incorrect EVM behavior that persists until application restart?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a `contracts.evm.version` value with a malformed format (e.g., `v999.999` or an empty string), causing `ConfigProviderImpl` to fail during `versionedConfiguration` initialization and producing an unhandled exception that crashes the application?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a `contracts.chainId` value of `0` or a negative number, causing the consensus node library to reject all contract calls with an invalid chain ID error and producing a denial of service for all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a `hedera.realm` value of `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing entity ID construction to overflow and produce incorrect entity lookups that expose data from unintended entities?
